originalAlarm.description: 1st function call argument is an uninitialized value

sanitizedDescription: 1st function call argument is an uninitialized value
model:
(define-fun __STRICT_ANSI__ () Bool
  true)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __malloc_and_calloc_defined () Bool
  false)
reasoning: The alarm claims `sc` (first argument to strcpy at L9 in 2a6cbfa00e0.c) is uninitialized. But sc is assigned at L6 from malloc(). The PC contains only restrictive library macros (!_STDLIB_H, !__malloc_and_calloc_defined) with no real feature flags — this is a desugaring artifact where stdlib.h internals are in a non-standard state, causing CSA to mistrack the malloc return value.
